title: Committed review catches overlap ownership gaps across lifecycle windows

provenance.originSummary: Synthetic benchmark scenario designed to exercise the rule directly.
provenance.whyItMattered: Use this case to benchmark deterministic overlap-surface coverage for grouped-management resources so reruns do not drift based on the active editor file or live repo shape.
provenance.genericCondition: Synthetic fixture models the historical drift where the active file is the new grouped-management resource, but the critical defect sits on an unchanged legacy single-item surface `internal/services/example/example_item_resource.go`. The deterministic review must still add that sibling surface because it can manage the same remote object.

description: A synthetic committed-review scenario where a new grouped-management resource is introduced, but the real defect lives on a pre-existing sibling surface that can still manage the same remote object through unguarded lifecycle windows. The correct review must inspect the unchanged sibling surface and surface the ownership-overlap gap regardless of which new file is active in the editor.

scope.notes: Synthetic fixture models the modeled drift where the active file is the new grouped-management resource, but the critical defect sits on an unchanged legacy single-item surface `internal/services/example/example_item_resource.go`. The deterministic review must still add that sibling surface because it can manage the same remote object.

mustCatch.description: The committed review should inspect unchanged sibling ownership surfaces when a new grouped-management resource overlaps existing management paths, rather than limiting review to the new files alone.

The committed review should check import/read/update/delete mode-gating symmetry on the overlapping sibling surface and surface a missing guard when create or update is protected but importer, read, or delete is not.

mustNotFlag.description: The review must not let the active new-resource file suppress inspection of unchanged overlapping sibling surfaces.

notes: Use this case to benchmark deterministic overlap-surface coverage for grouped-management resources so reruns do not drift based on the active editor file or live repo shape.
